_meta.title: scion deep-audit — adopt/adapt/avoid for M-Adapter-Modernization (containerisation + adapters)
_meta.method: 8-agent seed-blueprint workflow (runId w2bjaeug5): 7 area-auditors (D-img/D-boot/D-run/D-tmux/D-plugin/D-creds/D-coord) + 1 synthesizer; 916k tokens, 209 tool-calls
_meta.disposition: INSPIRATION not verbatim — respect scion license; no code lift. Decision-areas keyed D-img/D-boot/D-run/D-tmux/D-plugin/D-creds/D-coord.

headline_takeaways: Scion's 3-tier image cascade IS our fat-kernel/thin-shim split expressed in Docker layers, and is the strongest structural validation of our agreed architecture: heavy OS+toolchain base (rarely changes) -> org layer that multi-stage-bakes the shared binary + sets a PID-1 ENTRYPOINT -> a TINY per-harness leaf that only installs ONE agent CLI + sets CMD (image-build/gemini/Dockerfile is 14 lines; image-build/claude/Dockerfile leaf is a single npm install). One image per harness-TYPE; per-agent diffs (UID/creds/worktree) injected at runtime, never baked. Map our layers onto this directly.

The most expensive lesson is a NEGATIVE result that maps 1:1 to our shim decision: scion BUILT hashicorp/go-plugin RPC-subprocess plugins for harnesses and RIPPED THEM OUT (verified: pkg/plugin/ now has refbroker only, no refharness/harness_plugin.go). Verdict in .design/decoupled-harness-implementation.md: go-plugin is 'heavyweight... overkill for file-writing provisioning logic'; 'provisioning is a scripting problem, not a systems programming problem.' Our per-harness shim must be a declarative manifest + thin module, never process-isolation RPC. (They keep go-plugin only for genuinely long-running brokers.)

Scion's coordination is the exact CONTRAST and confirms our differentiator is net-new with nothing to reuse. It is PULL + HOST-EXTERNAL injection: the orchestrator execs `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer` into the live pane per message (pkg/agent/manager.go) with NO consumption ACK, papered over with 'two extra Enter keypresses 300ms apart to ensure the input is accepted' (manager.go:316-321) + a 2s debounce. Coordination liveness is coupled to the orchestrator's ability to exec into the container, so a Hub/orchestrator blip wedges delivery and nothing self-heals. Our kernel-IN-container must OWN injection with a real submit/ack so pushed messages survive blips by construction.

Scion has NO platform self-heal, which directly violates our pure-resilience pillar: K8s pods run RestartPolicyNever (k8s_runtime.go:1240), there is NO Restart verb (restart/update = Delete+Run driven by Go orchestrator code, run.go:60-106), and the 30s heartbeat only REPORTS liveness (heartbeat.go) — it does not restart crashed agents. We must put resilience INTO the system: kernel self-restart and/or native supervision, not external recreate.

Scion's reproducibility is HALF-built and shows exactly the gap we must close. It does the right STRUCTURE (dual-tag :latest + :<short-sha>, thread the immutable :sha as BASE_IMAGE for chained builds, ldflags-stamp GIT_COMMIT+BUILD_TIME, multi-stage lean images, content-hashed config bundles) but floats nearly every agent CLI/tool to `latest` (claude has NO pin at all; only antigravity pins). Adopt the structure, then PIN EVERY dependency to an exact version + digest-pin base images to satisfy our no-drift pillar.

Scion's telemetry is genuinely strong and directly reusable as our shim/kernel split applied to observability: a thin data-driven per-harness dialect (YAML event-name + dotted-path mappings) -> ONE shared event vocabulary -> ONE OTEL gen_ai.* pipeline + baked-in field-level redaction + file-touch swarm signal (pkg/sciontool/hooks/dialects/, handlers/telemetry.go). A new harness adds a dialect file, not pipeline code. Observe via structured hook-events, NEVER by screen-scraping the pane.
adopt: 3-tier layer cascade as the literal expression of fat-kernel/thin-shim: OS+toolchain base -> org layer that BAKES the shared @apnex/network-adapter kernel + sets the PID-1 ENTRYPOINT -> a tiny per-harness leaf that only `npm install`s the agent CLI + sets CMD. [D-img, D-plugin]

A single PID-1 init/supervisor binary as ENTRYPOINT that OWNS boot before the agent launches and runs as the long-running in-container supervisor: remap UID/GID -> run hooks -> assemble env -> exec child harness. This is the right home for the kernel + its resilient Hub connection. [D-boot, D-run]

One narrow runtime seam: ~12-verb Runtime interface (NO Restart) + ONE fat config struct, a factory with settings-driven + OS-autodetect selection + a null-object ErrorRuntime, and ALL runtime-agnostic logic in one helper (buildCommonRunArgs) with each backend a thin wrapper. [D-run, D-plugin]

Thin-wrapper-over-declarative-manifest per-harness adapter: ONE generic type implements the whole interface by reading a per-harness manifest (container_script_harness.go reads config.yaml); per-harness = data + a tiny module. Pair with one narrow harness interface + small OPTIONAL capability interfaces, a single resolver with an Implementation discriminator, and a typed 3-valued capability matrix (yes/partial/no + reason) so callers gate gracefully instead of silently no-op'ing. [D-plugin]

ENV-injection as the SINGLE universal config + per-agent parameterization contract, identical across backends (SCION_* prefix; identity via SCION_AGENT_NAME/TEMPLATE/BROKER). Per-agent identity configured from OUTSIDE with zero image changes. [D-boot, D-creds]

FAIL-CLOSED boot: a required pre-start provisioning failure ABORTS startup -> PhaseError, never launching a misconfigured child; and a fail-closed ValidateAuth gate (method set, no empty env values, every cred file exists on disk) BEFORE launch. Directly serves no-silent-wedge. [D-boot, D-creds]

tmux as the mandatory, uniform detached session on every runtime: fixed name `scion`, two-window (agent window 0 + a side `shell`) layout, `new-session -d` then attach, `set-option -g window-size latest`, attach/exec ALWAYS as the session-owner user. [D-tmux]

Deterministic teardown bound to the agent window: wrap the harness `sh -c '<cmd>; echo $? > file'` to capture the REAL exit code across the tmux grandchild boundary, plus a pane-exited hook -> kill-session -> supervisor shutdown -> container exits with the recorded code. [D-tmux]

Structured observability separate from the terminal: a hook-emitted event log + `tmux capture-pane` ONLY as a cheap non-interactive snapshot, never as the data path. Drive K8s PTY via the Go client's SPDY remotecommand API so no kubectl binary is needed in-image. [D-tmux]

Data-driven cross-harness telemetry normalization: thin per-harness dialect (YAML event-name + dotted field-paths) -> ONE shared event vocabulary -> ONE OTEL gen_ai.* pipeline (spans/metrics/correlated logs), with field-level redaction/hashing baked into the path and file-touch (file_path) captured as a first-class swarm/contention signal. [D-coord, D-plugin]

Build provenance stamped into the baked artifact via ldflags (GIT_COMMIT+BUILD_TIME with VCS auto-stamp disabled and SHA passed as a build-arg). Our analog: stamp kernel version + git SHA into the kernel so a running container self-reports provenance. [D-img, D-tmux]

Dual-tag every image :latest + :<short-sha> AND thread the IMMUTABLE :sha (not :latest) as BASE_IMAGE for any chained build in a run. Multi-stage builds (compile in builder, COPY only artifacts into a lean final). One build orchestrator over pluggable backends with a single-source-of-truth DAG so the SAME image builds for local-dev and CI/prod. [D-img, D-run]

Lean, secret-safe .dockerignore (excludes .git, *key*/*secret*/*.env, node_modules, vendor) and pre-create+chown runtime-state dirs at BUILD time so runtime ENV/secret mounts and config writes never hit root-owned parents at boot. [D-img, D-boot, D-creds]

Harness-AGNOSTIC 4-stage cred pipeline (gather env+probe well-known cred files -> overlay -> per-harness resolve preference-order -> fail-closed validate) as a shared-kernel resolver; per-harness is only a preference-order shim. [D-creds, D-plugin]

Token-from-FILE not env: write the Hub token to a 0600 file via atomic tmp+rename and DELETE the env var so every in-container process re-reads on refresh with NO restart; add on-demand credential-helper + proactive token refresh as a kernel-owned live-refresh loop. [D-creds, D-tmux]

Defense-by-ABSENCE isolation: never mount a sibling's secret-bearing state, relocate per-agent home/prompt/config OUT of any shared mount, keep per-agent git identity in $HOME/.gitconfig — because container UID == host UID, chmod gives zero isolation. [D-creds]

Content-addressed config/distribution: per-file SHA-256 + overall ContentHash, verified before install; entrypoint-time secret materialization (decode an injected blob in init and write 0600 files BEFORE any hook/harness runs); and the containment invariant — the trusted core STAGES per-harness code, the container EXECUTES it, the orchestrator NEVER runs untrusted provisioning code. [D-creds, D-img, D-boot, D-plugin]

Local-persist + external-converge status: write agent-info.json atomically (0644) so a heartbeat can relay the LAST status to the Hub even after the container exits — status survives container death. [D-coord]
adapt: Single-image-per-harness-TYPE shape is exactly ours, but REPLACE scion's entrypoint: scion starts root only to remap UID 1000 to the host user for bind-mounts. Swap in OUR entrypoint that ENV-injects per-agent config, boots the kernel, and launches the detached tmux session; keep root-start ONLY if we actually bind-mount host-owned worktrees. [D-img, D-boot, D-tmux]

INVERT the baked-vs-mounted boundary. Scion bakes the CLI binary but MOUNTS the provisioner (provision.py staged at start, not in the image). We BAKE the @apnex kernel (npm-as-source baked) so push-injection survival never depends on a runtime fetch; keep ONLY per-agent ENV/creds external; update = rebuild image + restart. Their mount-the-provisioner buys redeploy-without-rebuild at the cost of reproducibility — we prefer baked-kernel. [D-img, D-plugin, D-boot]

Centralize the pre-start provision CONTRACT in the shared KERNEL instead of N per-harness scripts. Scion re-implements auth-selection + universal->native MCP translation in Python per harness; we have a fat kernel, so implement that translation/auth-selection ONCE, invoked from the thin shim. Keep the 'pre-start hook that prepares native config + selects auth + fails closed' shape; make the shim a JS/TS module or declarative manifest, not a separate Python script. [D-plugin, D-boot]

Adapt the declarative per-harness manifest SHAPE (command base/resume/task flags, env_template, capability matrix, auth tables + autodetect, no_auth behavior) but with OUR fields, consumed by the kernel — not a contract driving a container-script, and not scion's vertex-ai/gcloud-adc specifics. [D-plugin, D-boot]

Immutable-recreate lifecycle is right, but move resilience INTO the system. Scion's recreate is Go-orchestrator code with RestartPolicyNever + report-only heartbeat = no platform self-heal. Use k8s-native RestartPolicy/supervision OR have OUR kernel self-restart so a crash/Hub-blip auto-recovers. [D-run]

Adapt message-injection mechanics, not the transport ownership: keep bracketed-paste (so TUI harnesses don't eat chars as hotkeys) and the harness-specific interrupt key, but OUR kernel owns delivery INSIDE the container with an explicit submit/ack + an ordered delivery queue — replacing scion's external per-message exec + 2s blind-timer debounce + 300ms double-Enter. [D-coord, D-tmux]

Adapt the heartbeat into kernel SELF-reporting that is part of a self-healing loop (detect wedge -> reconnect/restart), not passive report-only liveness. Adapt the liveness FSM's sticky semantics (waiting_for_input/completed resist being clobbered by trailing tool events) to fix our known false-idle reads. [D-run, D-coord]

Keep scion's no-poll self-report-and-wait scheduling spirit but ENFORCE it: back `status blocked` + pushed-notification with OUR lease-expiry as the structural stall-detector so an abandoned/wedged agent is reclaimed — scion is cooperative-only and reclaims nothing. [D-coord]

Adapt the K8s startup-gate (poll a readiness marker, then exec the real entrypoint) as a LIGHT entrypoint readiness gate. Since we bake config + inject via ENV, we can drop scion's home/workspace file-sync dance and start clean. [D-boot, D-img]

Adapt per-agent git worktree over a shared per-node base .git (first agent pays the clone, rest a cheap `worktree add --relative-paths`, dual-mount .git + worktree), but ALWAYS create the worktree host-side and mount it in — NEVER in-container (path-identity corruption). Use a refcounted sharer registry so teardown only fires on the last sharer. [D-creds, D-run]

Adapt scion's content-hash-verified, fail-closed two-phase download and apply that provenance verification to OUR image-pull — NOT to live in-container file re-pull (our update = rebuild + restart). Make hash verification MANDATORY (drop scion's skip-on-empty-hash legacy escape). [D-img, D-creds, D-run]

Adapt Hub-side lifecycle hooks (trigger taxonomy running/suspended/stopped/error; CAS exactly-once HA dedup; retry/backoff; SSRF guards; audit invariants that never log tokens/bodies) for OUR telemetry push-OUT to external systems, but DO NOT conflate them with our push-INTO-session coordination — they are different channels. [D-coord, D-run]

Adapt the Cloud-Build build/push split + local buildx layer cache ONLY for our HEAVY OS base; our short npm-kernel build can skip the split but keep the layer cache. Keep scion's dev-override bind-mount escape hatch (binaries at top of PATH) for kernel-dev velocity, but gate it behind an explicit dev flag so prod never silently shadows the baked kernel. [D-img, D-run]

Adapt the tmux status footer fed from injected ENV to show OUR agent identity + Hub endpoint + adapter-kernel version. Adapt a SCION_HOST_UID-style 'am I inside the container' env guard as a general in-container guard for the kernel/shim. [D-tmux, D-boot]

Adapt scion's stateless secret-blob-via-env transport, but use OUR push channel: since we HAVE a live coordination channel, PULL creds over it (or use a file-mount) to avoid the env-blob inspect/proc exposure window entirely — this is a net-new option scion couldn't take. [D-creds, D-coord]
Adapt scheduled/deferred Hub messages (--in/--at) as adjacent to our pulse primitive, but keep OUR cadence/ack/missedCount semantics rather than scion's fire-and-forget one-shot. [D-coord]
avoid: Floating `latest`/unpinned deps — the single biggest reproducibility hole and a direct violation of our no-drift pillar. claude has NO version pin; gemini/codex/copilot/hermes default `latest`; bases are oven/bun:latest + node:20-slim; kubectl from stable.txt; golangci-lint from HEAD. A rebuild of the SAME git SHA can yield different binaries. Pin EVERY dependency to an exact version and digest-pin base images. [D-img]

go-plugin / any RPC-subprocess for the per-harness ADAPTER. Scion built it and ripped it out as 'heavyweight... overkill for file-writing provisioning logic'. A declarative manifest + thin module is the correct weight class. [D-plugin]

Compiled-in per-harness implementations as the primary path AND baking per-harness-VARYING logic into the shared in-container binary — the 'dialect trap' where hook dialects compiled into the kernel forced a kernel+image rebuild for every new harness. Keep per-harness as data/shim; reserve image-rebuild for KERNEL changes. [D-plugin, D-img]

The entire scion coordination shape: PULL discovery + a host-EXTERNAL injector reaching INTO the container via Exec->tmux send-keys; keystroke-simulation as the coordination transport; timing-based delivery confirmation (300ms double-Enter); free-text-only payloads + a closed 5-value status enum as the entire coordination semantic surface; and screen-scraping (`scion look`) as a status/data source. This couples coordination liveness to orchestrator exec access (a blip wedges delivery, nothing self-heals) and puts semantics in the LLM's head. Our kernel owns injection with real ack/retry over typed coordination entities. [D-coord]

One-shot pods (RestartPolicyNever) + heartbeat-as-reporting-only + no auto-restart of crashed agents — the agent is recoverable only by an external recreate, which violates no-silent-wedge / survives-blips-by-construction. [D-run]

Shipping a runtime backend whose lifecycle is unimplemented: CloudRunRuntime.Run/Stop/Delete/List/Logs/Exec all return 'not yet implemented' while it is exposed as a host-type. Implement the FULL lifecycle behind the interface or don't expose the host-type. [D-run]

Gratuitous per-runtime duplication — scion's podman impl explicitly chose 'independent implementation (duplicate from Docker) for clean separation', trading our maximal-shared-core pillar for drift tolerance. Keep each per-runtime/per-harness piece a genuinely thin shim over the shared core. [D-run, D-plugin]

Live in-container file mutation as the UPDATE path (harness-config sync/pull/update writing into a running install) — hand-rebuild/config-drift risk. Bake config into the image; update = rebuild + restart. Likewise never treat mutable :latest as the deploy reference of record — deploy/record the immutable :sha/digest even if a :latest auto-pull triggers the restart. [D-img, D-run]

Ephemeral state that must survive restart but doesn't — scion's Hub uses SQLite on /tmp/scion.db, lost on cold start, mitigated only by a warm --min-instances 1. Our postgres substrate already avoids this; keep it that way. [D-run]

Relying on file permissions / per-agent UID for inter-agent isolation — container UID == host UID, so chmod 0700 protects nothing between agents. Isolate by ABSENCE (separate mounts/paths). Never create git worktrees in-container (path-identity corruption from --relative-paths). [D-creds, D-run]

Secret-as-env-blob exposure window (staged secrets visible in docker inspect / /proc/1/environ until init unsets it) and hash-optional/skip-verification back-compat escapes. Prefer file-mount or Hub-pull for secrets and make integrity verification mandatory. [D-creds, D-img]

Treating tmux as durable state and letting terminal-scrape become the data path — the session is in-memory and dies with the container (no checkpoint/restore), and flattening agent output to an opaque string causes content leaks + lossy downstream. Keep capture-pane strictly for observability. Also: never let exec/attach run as a non-owner of the tmux socket, and don't over-invest in multi-hop OSC52 clipboard relays. [D-tmux]

Monolithic heavy base bundling everything for every harness (core-base installs Go toolchain, gcloud, kubectl, gcsfuse, chromium, etc. into EVERY agent image) and duplicated install/repo-setup across stages instead of copy-from-builder. Keep the base minimal (node + tmux + kernel deps); push specialty tooling into only the leaves that need it; and keep build/non-config artifacts out of the config-identity hash. [D-img, D-plugin]

per_decision_guidance.guidance: ADOPT scion's 3-tier cascade in spirit: OS+toolchain base -> org layer that multi-stage-bakes the @apnex kernel + sets the PID-1 ENTRYPOINT + ldflags-stamps kernel version+SHA -> a tiny per-harness leaf that only installs the agent CLI. ADOPT dual-tag :latest + :<short-sha>, thread the IMMUTABLE :sha as BASE_IMAGE, multi-stage lean images, one orchestrator over pluggable backends, secret-safe .dockerignore, pre-chowned runtime dirs. CORRECTIVE: scion floats nearly everything to `latest` (claude has no pin) — pin EVERY dep + digest-pin bases so a rebuild of one SHA is byte-reproducible. Keep the base minimal. Deploy/record the immutable :sha/digest, never :latest as reference of record.

ADOPT a single PID-1 init/supervisor as ENTRYPOINT that owns boot, but make it OUR kernel-bootstrap (ENV-inject config -> boot kernel + open resilient Hub connection -> launch detached tmux -> exec harness), replacing scion's UID-remap-only init. ADOPT ENV-injection as the single config + per-agent contract, entrypoint-time secret materialization (write 0600 BEFORE any process runs), deterministic deep-merge precedence. ADOPT FAIL-CLOSED boot. Centralize the pre-start provision contract in the kernel, not N per-harness scripts. Keep any readiness gate LIGHT (drop scion's home/workspace file-sync dance).

ADOPT the narrow Runtime seam (~12 verbs + one config struct, factory with autodetect + null-object error runtime, all shared logic in one helper with thin per-backend wrappers) and the immutable-recreate model (update = new image + recreate; capture harness exit code to a file). CORRECTIVE for resilience: scion has NO self-heal (RestartPolicyNever, report-only heartbeat, no Restart verb, no reclaim) — put resilience IN the system via kernel self-restart and/or native supervision, backed by lease-expiry as the structural stall-detector. AVOID exposing any host-type you haven't fully implemented (CloudRunRuntime stub) and gratuitous per-runtime duplication. Commit to a minimum viable, fully-implemented runtime set (local+VM first) before claiming k8s/cloudrun.

ADOPT scion's proven tmux model wholesale: mandatory uniform wrapper across runtimes, fixed session name, two-window layout, new-session -d then attach, window-size latest, attach/exec ALWAYS as session-owner. ADOPT harness-exit-to-file wrapper + pane-exited -> kill-session teardown, capture-pane as cheap snapshot ONLY, SPDY-API PTY (no kubectl in-image), status footer fed from ENV. Treat tmux as NON-durable (dies with the container) — persist work in volume/Hub. CRITICAL DIVERGENCE: do NOT use tmux keystroke-injection as the coordination transport or rely on timed double-Enter 'acks'; injection is a transport detail OWNED by the in-container kernel with a real submit/ack. Observe via structured hook-events, never screen-scrape.

STRONGEST validation of our shim/kernel split: ADOPT thin-wrapper-over-declarative-manifest (one generic type reads a per-harness manifest), one narrow harness interface + small OPTIONAL capability interfaces, a single resolver with an Implementation discriminator, a typed 3-valued capability matrix (yes/partial/no + reason). ADOPT the containment invariant (trusted core STAGES per-harness code, container EXECUTES it). HARD AVOID: go-plugin/RPC for the adapter (scion built+removed it) and compiling per-harness-varying logic into the shared binary (the 'dialect trap'). ADAPT: make the shim a JS/TS module or declarative manifest (not Python), centralize universal->native MCP translation + auth-selection in the kernel, collapse scion's TWO onboarding paths into ONE self-contained bundle. NET-NEW beyond scion: the kernel must ALSO hold the live push channel open at runtime, not just write static MCP config at start.

ADOPT the harness-AGNOSTIC 4-stage cred pipeline as a shared-kernel resolver with per-harness = preference-order shim only. ADOPT token-from-FILE (0600 atomic rename, env var deleted) + on-demand credential-helper + proactive refresh as a kernel-owned live-refresh loop so a long detached session survives cred churn with NO restart. ADOPT defense-by-ABSENCE isolation (UID==host-UID makes chmod worthless). ADOPT content-addressed config bundles with MANDATORY hash verification. ADAPT per-agent worktree over shared base .git but ALWAYS host-side then mount (never in-container). NET-NEW: since we have a push channel, consider PULLING creds over it to avoid the env-blob inspect/proc exposure window.

per_decision_guidance.decision: D-coord (their coordination model as the CONTRAST + telemetry)
per_decision_guidance.guidance: This is the explicit contrast and confirms our differentiator is net-new. AVOID the whole scion model: 'less is more' LLM-discretionary coordination (no roles/RACI/work-queue/leases/gates), free-text payloads + closed 5-value status enum as the entire semantic surface, host-EXTERNAL per-message injection via Exec->tmux send-keys with NO ack, screen-scraping as status. Its cost is visible in coordinator.md's graveyard of brittle workarounds. Instead: keep our first-class STRUCTURED coordination (typed work-claims/leases/pulses/RACI-routed notifications/gates) with injection OWNED by the resilient in-container kernel (real submit/ack/retry, ordered queue). DO ADOPT scion's telemetry: data-driven per-harness dialect -> one OTEL gen_ai.* pipeline, baked-in redaction, file-touch swarm signal, local-persist+external-converge status that survives container death; ENFORCE the cooperative no-poll pattern with lease-expiry reclaim.

divergences: Coordination model: scion is deliberately structure-LESS ('less is more / let the models decide how to coordinate') — no roles/RACI, no work-queue, no leases, no gates, no teleology; coordination is emergent LLM prose. Antithesis of our structured coordination. Precise framing: NOT that 'they cannot push' (they DO inject via tmux + push state-change notifications) — it is that ours is STRUCTURED + kernel-owned + ack'd over typed entities, while theirs is dumb-text, host-external, no-ACK, semantics living only in the LLM's head.

Injection ownership conflicts with pillar-1: scion's injector is the HOST orchestrator exec-ing into the live pane per message. Coordination liveness coupled to orchestrator exec access — a blip wedges delivery, nothing self-heals. Our kernel-IN-container must own injection so pushed messages survive blips by construction.

Resilience conflicts with pure-resilience-by-construction: scion has NO platform self-heal (RestartPolicyNever; heartbeat reports-only; no Restart verb; cooperative-only with NO reclaim; Hub SQLite on /tmp lost on cold start). We require kernel/native self-restart + lease-expiry reclaim.

Reproducibility conflicts with our versioned/no-drift pillar: scion floats nearly every agent CLI/tool to `latest` (claude unpinned) and treats live in-container file mutation as a valid update path. We pin everything + digest-pin bases, and update = rebuild image + restart only.

Maximal-shared-core: scion currently forks per-harness logic (N provision.py re-implementing auth + MCP translation) and deliberately duplicates per-runtime (podman 'duplicate from Docker'). NOTE the TRAJECTORY agrees with us — scion shrank its compiled harness set to {claude,gemini} and moved the rest to data-driven bundles, and abandoned RPC plugins — so its end-state direction matches our fat-kernel/thin-shim even where current state diverges.

Baked-vs-mounted boundary: scion bakes the CLI but MOUNTS the provisioner (redeploy-without-rebuild) — optimizing redeploy convenience at the cost of reproducibility + push-survival. We invert it (bake the kernel; only ENV/creds external), optimizing resilience + reproducibility over redeploy convenience.
open_questions: Resilience authority (D-run): where does self-heal live — kernel self-restart/self-supervision INSIDE the container, platform-native supervision (k8s Deployment/RestartPolicy, systemd, watchtower), or both layered? And how does it interact with our lease-expiry stall-detector so the two don't fight?

Cross-harness target scope (D-plugin): which harnesses do we actually target first (claude-only, or claude+opencode+gemini+codex)? This decides whether we need the full dialect + 3-valued capability-matrix machinery now or can defer it.
Shim form/language (D-plugin): is the per-harness shim a declarative manifest ONLY, or a manifest + a thin JS/TS adapter module loaded by the kernel? How much belongs in data vs code?

Secret transport (D-creds/D-coord): given we HAVE a push channel, do we PULL creds over the live coordination channel (eliminating the env-blob exposure window) versus a file-mount or staged-env-blob?

Tool-surface + push relationship (D-plugin/D-coord): we need BOTH static native MCP registration AND a live kernel-held push channel — is our tool surface an MCP server the kernel hosts, and how does push-injection relate to the harness's MCP/tool plumbing without colliding with TUI input?
Runtime target set + sequencing (D-run): what is our minimum viable, FULLY-implemented host-type set (local + VM first?), and do we commit to k8s/cloudrun later?

Observability transport (D-tmux/D-coord): do we ride telemetry on the same reconnecting channel as push-coordination, or keep them separate? Our own memory note warns against piggybacking coordination on a lossy notification pipeline.
